extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the final sprint in Rome after VIVIANI Elia's leadout train disintegrated inside 200m, while FROOME Chris sealed overall victory.
tactical_patterns: BENNETT Sam positioned fourth wheel then exploited final 150m acceleration advantage when VIVIANI committed early from SABATINI's leadout.
Quick-Step deployed three-man train for VIVIANI but PEDERSEN Mads disrupted positioning, causing MØRKØV Michael to pull VIVIANI off-line.
FROOME Chris reversed Sky's normal front-domination tactic, racing defensively from behind after arriving under-conditioned then attacking late stage 19.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: demonstrated superior top-end speed over 120m when correctly positioned, solving earlier positional deficits that cost him stages
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: won points jersey but leadout train fractured under pressure when SABATINI Fabio delivered him too early at 150m
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: first British Giro winner by abandoning Sky's pace-domination model, attacking from deficit position successfully
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: deliberately disrupted Quick-Step's sprint train organization, pulling MØRKØV Michael out of leadout sequence
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: negative: failed to hold leadout lane under pressure from PEDERSEN, exposing VIVIANI to jostling and early commitment
